rationale: Keep this pair because the cited paper is a close theoretical/mechanism predecessor: it studies attraction/compromise contexts and context-specific choice construction, which directly overlaps the citing paper’s focus on decoy-driven context effects and mechanism explanations (weight/similarity vs value-shift). The citing note explicitly frames itself as extending prior attraction/phantom-decoy work, so this 2008 JMR article is plausibly more than a passing mention even though the raw evidence here is only a DOI match.
